disclaimer: Entry rules can change. Always verify with official immigration or embassy sources before booking.

visa.notes: French passport holders enter Malaysia visa-free for up to 90 days per visit. No extension available — you must exit and re-enter. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months. Immigration officers may ask for proof of onward travel.
visa.verify_note: Confirm current policy at imi.gov.my or the Malaysian embassy before travel.

insurance.notes: Not required but strongly recommended. Malaysian private hospitals are excellent but expensive for foreigners. Public hospitals are cheaper but slower. Ensure cover for adventure activities if visiting Borneo or doing jungle trekking.

regions.notes: West coast sheltered from northeast monsoon. Can rain year-round but showers are short.
Many resorts close Nov–Feb due to northeast monsoon. Best diving Mar–Sep.
Rain year-round but heaviest Oct–Feb. Climbing Mt Kinabalu requires permits booked months ahead.

budget.verify_note: Exchange rate is approximate. Check xe.com before travel.

budget.label: Backpacker
budget.includes: Dorm bed, hawker stalls 3x, bus/LRT, 1 beer or teh tarik

mid.label: Comfort
mid.includes: Budget private room, mix of restaurants, Grab rides, activities

useful_apps.name: Grab
useful_apps.use: Dominant ride-hailing and food delivery app

useful_apps.name: Google Maps
useful_apps.use: Navigation and transit directions in cities

useful_apps.name: 12Go Asia
useful_apps.use: Bus and ferry bookings across Malaysia

transport: Kuala Lumpur has a solid rail network: LRT, MRT, KTM Komuter and the KLIA Ekspres airport train. Grab the Touch 'n Go eWallet app for seamless payments.
Grab is the go-to app for taxis — always use it over hailing cabs to avoid meter disputes.
Intercity buses are cheap and comfortable — book via Easybook or 12Go Asia.
Penang is walkable in Georgetown; rent a bicycle for island exploration.
For Borneo, internal flights are necessary — AirAsia and MASwings connect Kota Kinabalu, Kuching and interior towns.
Ferry services connect Langkawi, Penang and the Perhentian Islands — check seasonal schedules as routes may pause during monsoon.

scams.name: Taxi meter refusal
scams.description: Some KL taxis quote fixed (inflated) prices instead of using the meter. Use Grab instead — problem largely solved.

scams.name: Money changer short-change
scams.description: Unlicensed changers in tourist areas may count notes quickly or palm some. Use licensed changers in shopping malls and count carefully.

scams.name: Fake police
scams.description: Plain-clothes individuals claim to be police and demand to see your passport or wallet. Politely refuse and walk to a public area or hotel. Real officers will have ID.

scams.name: Overpriced tourist 'fixed price' restaurants
scams.description: Menus near Petronas Towers sometimes charge tourist prices with no indication. Check the menu before sitting.

emergency.notes: 999 covers police, ambulance and fire. The Tourist Hotline (1300-88-5050) assists with complaints and emergencies during office hours.

checklist.item: Passport valid 6+ months beyond departure date
Confirm visa exemption terms at imi.gov.my
Purchase travel insurance covering medical and evacuation
Save or print proof of onward travel — may be asked at immigration
Book Perhentian / Tioman accommodation early (Apr–Aug fills fast)
Buy Airalo eSIM or get a local SIM (Maxis/Celcom at airport)
Note that alcohol is expensive and unavailable in some areas (Kelantan)
Respect dress codes at mosques and temples — bring a sarong
Book Mt Kinabalu permits months in advance if trekking in Sabah
